message:
test: harden MCP auto-flush failure test + fix clippy on hex test

Two small testing-infra adjustments from concurrent agent work:

- src/mcp/mod.rs: with_mutation_reports_auto_flush_failure_and_
  preserves_dirty_state used to rely on an unwriteable jsonl path
  ("missing-parent/issues.jsonl") to provoke the auto-flush failure,
  then asserted the file did not exist afterwards. That shape broke
  on platforms where the parent was auto-created. New shape: pre-seed
  the jsonl with a literal git merge-conflict-marker document, so the
  auto-flush fails parsing it as valid JSONL, and assert the conflict
  markers are still on disk afterwards. Same invariant (dirty state
  preserved, flush did not clobber), more robust provocation.

- src/util/hash.rs: test_hex_encode_length_invariant's range adapter
  was `(0..len).map(|i| i as u8)` with len up to 255, which clippy's
  incompatible_cast flags because the iterator yields i32 and `as u8`
  truncates silently. Tightened to `map(|i: u32| u8::try_from(i).unwrap_or(0))`
  — the .unwrap_or branch is unreachable for len ≤ 255 but keeps the
  test panic-free if someone extends the range list later.

No production code touched.
